behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route no more than 1.5x optimal for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is algorithm quality in the heuristic itself, reproducible with fixed coordinate inputs in one local algorithm boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets heuristic quality directly, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function with deterministic coordinate fixtures (and seeded random sets), compute exact optimal length for those fixtures, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: Delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from distance between consecutive stops and configured average speed for road type.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic formula/config logic and can fail within one calculation boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text is about numeric computation from provided distance and speed config, not service-to-service handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the estimator with explicit segment distances and road-type speed config; assert per-segment and total time windows match expected values (including rounding/edge cases).

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects routes whose total estimated time exceeds remaining shift hours and returns the excess.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local validation/arithmetic behavior. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text isolates validator output (reject + excess amount), which is reproducible with direct inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator with total_estimated_time and remaining_shift_hours values around threshold; assert rejection and exact excess for over-limit cases.

behaviors.description: If one address fails geocoding (API returns no results), service skips that stop, optimizes remaining addresses, and reports skipped addresses.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at module boundaries (geocoding result handling -> optimizer input -> reporting), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP required because a deterministic in-process geocoder stub returning 'no results' reproduces the exact branch. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not inherently hide this orchestration bug if modules are wired. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified by behavior text 'API returns no results' (content case), not network/timeout/transport behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a fake geocoder module that returns coordinates for most addresses and no-results for one; assert optimized route excludes failed address and response includes that address in skipped list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses are consolidated into one stop with combined delivery items and not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on handoff between deduplication/aggregation and route planning output ('not visited twice'), which is a cross-module state consistency failure. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text concerns in-process data consolidation and route construction, not real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run service pipeline with input containing duplicate addresses and distinct items; assert output route has one stop for that address, items are merged, and route sequence contains no duplicate visit for it.
